metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Every builder of meritocratic hiring faces two questions: proving a candidate is real, and sorting average portfolios. DevProof's value proposition is making competence verifiable instead of inferred. A built, documented OAuth library is stronger evidence than a credential like 'MIT Computer Science 24.' Structural barriers keep credentials winning, and degraded signals push companies back to referral-only hiring.

artifactLines: Every builder of meritocratic hiring faces two questions: proving a candidate is real, and sorting average portfolios.
DevProof's value proposition is making competence verifiable instead of inferred.
A built, documented OAuth library is stronger evidence than a credential like 'MIT Computer Science 24.'
Structural barriers keep credentials winning, and degraded signals push companies back to referral-only hiring.

beats.narration_text: DevProof's claim that it makes competence 'verifiable instead of inferred' reframed hiring's two hard questions for me: proving a candidate is real, and sorting hundreds of average portfolios. I want to test it on the OAuth-library example. For Candidate A, the solo dev with the elegant zero-dependency library, (1) describe what verified evidence beats 'MIT Computer Science 24' here, (2) name the structural barrier that still lets the credential win, and (3) explain why degraded signals push firms back to referral-only hiring.

beats.narration_text: DevProof’s value proposition is: “We’ve made competence verifiable instead of inferred.” Candidate A: Solo developer who built a small, elegant OAuth library with exceptional documentation and zero dependencies.

beats.narration_text: They’re the structural barriers that have kept us locked in a system where “MIT Computer Science ‘24” means more than “built a production OAuth implementation.” They’re why companies still retreat to referral-only hiring when traditional signals degrade. They’re why we keep pretending that a 3.8 GPA tells us anything meaningful about whether someone can architect a distributed system.

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.
